content:
CORRECTION — EXPO SESSION OPENS, APP CRASHES AFTER CONNECTION

Expo Go successfully opens this project URL:

exp://neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt

Therefore, do not report that the QR code or Expo URL is missing.

The actual failure happens AFTER Expo Go connects to the project.

Audit this exact sequence now:

1. Confirm the loca.lt tunnel is still active and serving the Expo manifest.
2. Confirm Metro remains running after the phone connects.
3. Capture the Metro terminal output at the exact time Expo Go shows “Something went wrong.”
4. Open and capture the complete Expo Go “View error log.”
5. Verify the Android JavaScript bundle finishes successfully.
6. Identify the exact runtime exception, missing module, incompatible native dependency, or connection failure.
7. Test with a fresh tunnel session.
8. Also test using:
   - Expo tunnel
   - LAN mode if available
   - a development build if Expo Go has unsupported native modules
9. Do not mark the project working only because the exp:// URL opens.
10. The app is verified only when the real IVX interface renders and owner sign-in works.

REQUIRED PROOF:

A. Expo manifest request result
B. Metro connection log
C. Android bundle result
D. Exact Expo Go error log
E. Exact file and line causing failure
F. Screenshot showing the IVX app rendered
G. Owner sign-in test
H. Final status:

EXPO_SESSION_CONNECTED_APP_RENDERED

or

EXPO_SESSION_CONNECTED_RUNTIME_FAILED_WITH_EXACT_ERROR
